results.title: Open Weight Models Are Turning Inference Into A Control Point - Forbes
results.content: AI infrastructure firm Fireworks recently secured a $1.5 billion Series D at a $17.5 billion valuation, leading a wave of significant investments in companies providing managed services for open-weight and custom AI models. Alongside Baseten and Together AI, these firms have collectively raised nearly $4 billion in weeks, aiming to offer more cost-effective inference solutions than proprietary frontier APIs. They specialize in handling the complex operational aspects of deploying AI models, enabling businesses to customize and run models without managing their own GPUs. However, challenges loom, including competition from hyperscalers, the persistent performance gap with leading closed models, and undisclosed gross margins. Three companies in that business have raised roughly $3.8 billion between them in under four weeks. All three compete in managed infrastructure for training, adapting or serving open-weight and custom models, with inference at the center of their pitches. Their shared economic proposition is to run a customer’s own model in production for less than a frontier API would charge.

results.title: Gartner predicts AI platforms and models will swell this year - Telecoms
results.content: # Gartner predicts AI platforms and models will swell this year. Analyst Gartner is forecasting that globally the market for AI platforms and models will grow 63% this year. It reckons worldwide end-user spending on AI models and platforms will come in at $64 billion in 2026, up 63.4% from the $39 billion clocked in 2025. Spending on GenAI models is forecast to grow 117%, and AI platform spending by 36.9% this year. The analyst adds that it believes the biggest winners will be vendors that help enterprises manage where and how AI is used. Cheparthi added: “Over the long-term, the biggest winners will be vendors that help enterprises manage where and how AI is used across the business. As more models enter the market and usage-based pricing becomes harder to predict, buyers will turn to platforms that help them choose the right tools, monitor performance, enforce policy and keep costs under control.”.

results.title: KLAS Arch Collaborative 2026 Report: Early Insights into Epic AI Adoption and Clinician Experience - HIT Consultant
results.content: # KLAS Arch Collaborative 2026 Report: Early Insights into Epic AI Adoption and Clinician Experience. * Early metrics from the *KLAS Arch Collaborative 2026 report* confirm that clinicians using at least one artificial intelligence tool score 7 percentage points higher in believing the EHR enables operational efficiency compared to non-users. * A massive preparedness chasm exists across enterprise networks: less than 25% of clinicians who have adopted AI tools agree they received adequate training on managing AI-generated content within their active workflows. * The largest positive efficiency spreads are captured by clinicians weaponizing automation for focused operational tasks, led by order creation (+9%) and summarizing shifts or patient stays (+6%). According to the KLAS Arch Collaborative 2026 report, titled *AI Insights from the Arch Collaborative 2026: An Early Look at How Epic Customers Are Leveraging AI to Improve the Clinician Experience*, the conversation must urgently transition from basic technological deployment to role-specific workflow enablement.

results.title: Americans hold favorable views on AI benefits despite concerns - Idaho Business Review

Talk of artificial intelligence replacing workers, concerns over data centers and the potential for economic upheaval due to the technology have not prevented many Americans from viewing AI in a favorable light. “The American public is actually quite optimistic about the benefits AI will bring to their lives ― from medical breakthroughs to economic growth,” said Nathan Leamer, executive director of Build American AI. results.title: Global M&A hits $2.8T in H1 2026, AI and mega-deals lead sur - MarketScale
results.content: # Global M&A hits $2.8 trillion in H1 2026, with mega-deals and AI infrastructure pulling industrial manufacturing to a 28% surge. Global M&A activity reached a record $2.8 trillion in H1 2026, up 48% compared to the previous year. The increase was driven by trends like AI infrastructure and grid modernization. These factors contributed to a 28% surge in the industrial manufacturing sector. Global M&A activity in H1 2026 reached $2.8 trillion, marking a 48% increase from the previous year. AI infrastructure and grid modernization are significant drivers of the increased M&A activity. Global merger and acquisition activity hit a record $2.8 trillion in the first half of 2026, up 48% year-on-year and the highest year-to-date total since LSEG began tracking deals in 1980, according to Reuters. The common thread across both datasets is the same: AI infrastructure, grid modernization, and defense-linked assets are pulling capital at a pace that is rewriting what counts as a normal deal.

results.title: Layoffs 2026: List of Companies That Laid Off Massive Workforce Due to AI - LatestLY
results.content: # Layoffs 2026: List of Companies That Laid Off Massive Workforce Due to AI. ## Major global companies continue to cut thousands of jobs in 2026, with nearly 40 firms confirming layoffs due to AI integration, economic shifts, and restructuring. The global corporate landscape is witnessing a sustained period of workforce contraction throughout 2026, with nearly 40 major organisations announcing significant layoffs within the first half of the year.
